title: Use geometric sequence formulas
description: Given the formula of a geometric sequence, either in explicit form or in recursive form, find a specific term in the sequence.

assessment_items.question_content:
$a(n) = \dfrac{3}{2} \left(-2\right)^{n - 1}$

**What is the $3^\text{rd}$ term in the sequence?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
This is an explicit formula.

All we have to do is plug $n=3$ in the formula to find the $3^\text{rd}$ term.

assessment_items.question_content:
$b(n) = -1 \left(2\right)^{n - 1}$

** What is the $5^\text{th}$ term in the sequence?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
This is an explicit formula.

All we have to do is plug $n=5$ in the formula to find the $5^\text{th}$ term.

assessment_items.question_content:
$c(n) = -\dfrac{9}{2} \left(-\dfrac{4}{3}\right)^{n - 1}$

** What is the $2^\text{nd}$ term in the sequence?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
This is an explicit formula.

All we have to do is plug $n=2$ in the formula to find the $2^\text{nd}$ term.

assessment_items.question_content:

assessment_items.question_content:
$d(n) = 3 \left(-2\right)^{n - 1}$

** What is the $4^\text{th}$ term in the sequence?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
This is an explicit formula.

All we have to do is plug $n=4$ in the formula to find the $4^\text{th}$ term.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}a(1)=20\\\\
a(n)=a(n-1)\cdot\dfrac32
\end{cases}$

**What is the $3^{\text{rd}}$ term in the sequence?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $20$ and that the common ratio is $\dfrac32$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}b(1)=15\\\\
b(n)=b(n-1)\cdot (-3)
\end{cases}$

**What is the $4^{\text{th}}$ term in the sequence?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $15$ and that the common ratio is $-3$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}c(1)=\dfrac{3}{16}\\\\
c(n)=c(n-1)\cdot 4
\end{cases}$

**What is the $3^{\text{rd}}$ term in the sequence?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $\dfrac{3}{16}$ and that the common ratio is $4$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}d(1)=\dfrac{1}{12}\\\\
d(n)=d(n-1)\cdot (-6)
\end{cases}$

**What is the $4^{\text{th}}$ term in the sequence?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $\dfrac{1}{12}$ and that the common ratio is $-6$.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $-11$ and that the common ratio is $10$.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{cases}b(1)=-500\\\\
b(n)=b(n-1)\cdot \dfrac{4}{5}

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints: This is a recursive formula. It tells us that the first term is $-500$ and that the common ratio is $\dfrac{4}{5}$.

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
